name: start_recording
description: Start a screen recording (Windows gdigrab) as a background ffmpeg process. Returns a session id and output path. Recording is explicit and never auto-starts. Audio is not captured in Phase 1 (video only). A window: target records the on-screen rectangle the window occupies, resolved once at start (window must be visible, on top, not minimized); it does not follow a window moved/resized mid-recording.

target.description: full | monitor:<index> (0 = primary) | window:<title> | region:<x>,<y>,<w>,<h>. window: captures the rectangle the window occupies as displayed (case-insensitive exact title, else substring; topmost match)

name: stop_recording
description: Stop a recording by session id. Sends ffmpeg a graceful quit so the file is finalized rather than truncated, then returns the final path and duration.

name: list_sessions
description: List all recording sessions (active and finished) with their status, target, output path, and whether the process is still alive.

name: get_session
description: Inspect a single recording session by id, including live status and whether its output file exists on disk.

name: screenshot
description: Capture a single still frame (PNG) of a target (full screen, a monitor, a window, or a region). Returns the image path. A window: target captures the on-screen rectangle the window occupies as currently displayed (it must be visible, on top, and not minimized).

target.description: full | monitor:<index> | window:<title> | region:<x>,<y>,<w>,<h>. window: captures the rectangle the window occupies as displayed (case-insensitive exact title, else substring; topmost match)

name: sample_frames
description: Sample frames from a video so the agent can view what happened: either at a fixed rate (fps) or at explicit timestamps. Writes PNG frames to a folder and returns their paths.

input.description: Path to the source video file

fps.description: Sample this many frames per second (mutually exclusive with timestamps)

timestamps.description: Explicit second offsets to grab one frame at each (mutually exclusive with fps)

outputDir.description: Optional folder for the frames (defaults under SCREENCAST_HOME/frames)

name: get_media_info
description: Probe a media file with ffprobe and return duration, resolution, frame rate, codecs, container format, and size.

input.description: Path to the media file to probe

name: trim
description: Trim a video to a sub-clip by start + (end or duration). Uses stream copy for speed; cut points snap to the nearest keyframe.

end.description: End time in seconds (use end OR duration)

duration.description: Clip length in seconds (use end OR duration)

name: concat
description: Concatenate two or more videos into a single file using the ffmpeg concat demuxer (stream copy). Inputs should share the same codec and format.

inputs.description: Two or more video paths to join, in order

name: convert
description: Convert a video between mp4, gif, and webm. mp4 uses H.264, webm uses VP9, and gif uses a palette filter for quality. Returns the output path.

width.description: Output width in px, height auto (gif only; default 720)
